US 312 (1974)] where the learned Judge illustrates the 
said aspect by giving the example of a candidate coming 
from disadvantaged sections of society and yet obtaining 
reasonably good scores — thus manifesting his “promise 
and potential” — vis-a-vis a candidate from a higher strata 
[2025] 4 S.C.R.  
311
In Re: Recruitment of Visually Impaired in Judicial Services
obtaining higher scores. (His opinion is referred to in para 
716.) On account of the disadvantages suffered by them 
and the lack of opportunities, — the respondents say — 
members of backward classes of citizens may not score 
equally with the members of socially advanced classes at 
the inception but in course of time, they would. It would be 
fallacious to presume that nature has endowed intelligence 
only to the members of the forward classes. It is to be 
found everywhere. It only requires an opportunity to prove 
itself. The directive in Article 46 must be understood and
